name: OpenAI o3-mini (High)
note: Highest reasoning-effort setting: best accuracy of the o3-mini family, higher latency/cost per query.

results.notes: From OpenAI's own simple-evals benchmark-harness README table (o3-mini-high row).

GDPval-AA v2, independently measured by Artificial Analysis on the 'o3-mini (high)' model page. Genuine floor result (0%, last place of 28 models on this eval) -- an older reasoning model with no dedicated real-world-agentic-work tuning. Not previously tracked here. [score corrected during round-6 completeness verify: proposed 0 -> 468] [score corrected during round-12 accuracy re-verify: 468 -> 0, per verifier: Loaded artificialanalysis.ai/models/o3-mini-high in a real browser (WebFetch's text extraction misses the JS-rendered benchmark bars). The GDPval-AA v2 bar chart on that exact page lists 'o3-mini (high)' in last place among 29 models with a score of ] [SCALE FIX 2026-07-17: corrected from a normalized percentage (0%) back to this taxonomy's actual raw 0-2000-point Elo scale. This model's own note already documents an earlier round-6 finding of raw=468 that a later round-12 adversarial re-verify wrongly 'corrected' back down to a percentage, not realizing this benchmark's dominant corpus convention is raw Elo, not a 0-100 normalized score. Restoring the original round-6 raw reading.]
Terminal-Bench v2.1, independently measured by Artificial Analysis on the 'o3-mini (high)' model page. Not previously tracked here.

Humanity's Last Exam, independently measured by Artificial Analysis on the 'o3-mini (high)' model page. Not previously tracked on this catalog entry (existing results are all from LessWrong/simple-evals, neither of which covers HLE for this model).
IFBench (instruction following), independently measured by Artificial Analysis on the 'o3-mini (high)' model page. Not previously tracked here.

Sibling rows 'O3-Mini-2025-01-31 (Med)' at 67.4->lower and '(Low)' also present in the same table and rank below High, consistent with the corpus's existing High>Med>Low ordering for this model family on AIME/GPQA/Codeforces/SWE-bench.

verificationNotes:
Existence, release date (2025-01-31), 200K context and $1.10/$4.40 pricing all confirmed via independent sources (VentureBeat, OpenAI docs mirrors). All 4 benchmark rows (AIME 87.3, GPQA 79.7, Codeforces 2130, SWE-bench 49.3) confirmed verbatim on the cited LessWrong post. No changes made.

[2026-07-16] Added 'livecodebench' (67.4) sourced from the live LiveCodeBench leaderboard (see result notes). Checked 'mmmu' against (1) OpenAI's official o3-mini system card PDF (cdn.openai.com/o3-mini-system-card-feb10.pdf, full-text searched: no MMMU or multimodal-benchmark mention) and (2) prompthub.us's o3-mini model card, which states explicitly 'o3-mini does not support vision capabilities' -- corroborated by this corpus's own capabilities.vision=false field and independent reporting (simonwillison.net, GitHub simonw/llm#1037) that o3-mini is text-only with no image input. MMMU is a multimodal/vision-category benchmark (see corpus/benchmarks.json); a text-only model cannot be evaluated on it, so the absence is architectural, not a reporting gap -- omitted rather than guessed.
